text: Handling deadlines effectively is a key skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break a large project into smaller tasks, which makes it less overwhelming. Prioritization is equally important: decide which tasks are urgent and do them first, leaving less critical work for later. Scope management means resisting the temptation to add extra features or details at the last minute; stick to the original plan. Finally, communication is vital—if you see a problem, let your team or boss know early. These strategies reduce stress and help you deliver quality work on time.
